refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. It is solution-neutral as it does not specify how latency is achieved, only that it must be compatible with safe operation. The term 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize the safety requirement without prescribing a specific latency value or technology, maintaining problem domain focus.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that it meets the safety criteria defined by stakeholders, such as latency p95 ≤ 150 ms.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate adaptive latency monitoring and dynamic adjustment mechanisms
Separate teleoperation control data from non-critical data streams to reduce congestion
Use predictive control algorithms to compensate for unavoidable latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication protocol and control algorithms
Shared resources causing delays in data processing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on the communication protocol layer, which can be designed independently from control algorithms and other system functions, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency requirements
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Latency measurement and monitoring are accurate and timely
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive latency monitoring and dynamic adjustment as a complementary DP to enhance robustness against variable network conditions.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for remote control and communication between operator and vehicle

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data flow for vehicle status and control feedback

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and instructions from operator to vehicle

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Underlying network connection used for communication protocol

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many unrelated functions, leading to high diffusion of communication protocol logic.
Vehicle module handles local systems; communication protocol partially relevant but some diffusion to Teleoperation expected.
Most specific module responsible for communication and remote control; low diffusion expected outside this boundary.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; communication protocol is peripheral, leading to poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the low-latency communication protocol DP because it directly manages remote control and communication functions, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for telemetry data may cause some coupling.
Network Link interface may require coordination with broader system modules, risking slight diffusion.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If diffusion with Vehicle or Network Link is problematic, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate protocol logic from vehicle-specific telemetry handling to improve modular containment.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer, which aligns with the requirement to ensure safe remote operation with latency p95 ≤ 150 ms.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for telemetry data may cause some coupling.
Network Link interface may require coordination with broader system modules, risking slight diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), making it the most appropriate IM boundary for the DP. However, some diffusion risk exists due to dependencies on Vehicle and Network Link modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through latency measurements is feasible within the Teleoperation module by monitoring end-to-end communication latency under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Teleoperation IM is clear and appropriate, as this module is responsible for remote control and communication, directly supporting the FR.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to explicitly separate protocol logic from vehicle-specific telemetry handling to reduce coupling and improve modular containment.
Consider defining clearer interface contracts with Vehicle and Network Link modules to minimize diffusion and dependencies.
Evaluate if any communication protocol components currently spread across multiple modules can be centralized within the Teleoperation module to reduce diffusion risk.

decision.justification: Although all gates passed, the recommended revisions highlight important improvements to reduce coupling and diffusion risks, which is critical given the high safety criticality. Refining the DP to separate protocol logic from vehicle telemetry handling and clarifying interface contracts will enhance modular containment and reduce risk. Centralizing communication protocol components within the Teleoperation module will further minimize diffusion. Addressing these revisions now will strengthen the design before proceeding to next-level sub-FRs.

revise.why: To explicitly separate protocol logic from vehicle-specific telemetry handling, define clearer interface contracts with Vehicle and Network Link modules, and centralize communication protocol components within the Teleoperation module to reduce coupling and diffusion risks.

assumptions: The current DP and IM mapping sufficiently addresses the FR but can be improved for modularity and risk reduction.
Verification of latency is feasible within the Teleoperation module under agreed network conditions.
open_risks: Potential coupling due to dependency on Vehicle module for telemetry data.
Diffusion risk from Network Link interface coordination with broader system modules.
evidence_needed: Demonstration that refined DP reduces coupling and diffusion.
Verification plan showing latency measurements within Teleoperation module.
Interface contracts with Vehicle and Network Link modules to confirm minimized dependencies.
